slides.title: PhD CET Mock Exam 10 - Research Methodology
slides.subtitle: 50 Practice MCQs for Doctoral Entrance Examination • Topic 1-5

slides.question: Q1: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Case Study Method**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a qualitative approach that focuses on describing the lived experiences of individuals regarding a specific concept or phenomenon.
It is equivalent in purpose to: focuses on gathering numerical data and generalizing it across groups of people or to explain a particular phenomenon.
It is correctly described as: is an in-depth, detailed study of an individual, group, or event to explore underlying principles in a real-life context.
It has no relation to: in-depth single unit analysis
slides.explanation: Correct! **Case Study Method** is accurately described as: is an in-depth, detailed study of an individual, group, or event to explore underlying principles in a real-life context.

slides.question: Q2: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Experimental Research**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is exploratory research used to gain an understanding of underlying reasons, opinions, and motivations.
It has no relation to: cause-and-effect verification
It is correctly described as: establishes cause-and-effect relationships by manipulating an independent variable and observing its effect on a dependent variable.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a systematic qualitative methodology that involves the generation of theory from systematic analysis of data.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Experimental Research** is accurately described as: establishes cause-and-effect relationships by manipulating an independent variable and observing its effect on a dependent variable.

slides.question: Q3: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Diagnostic Research**?
options: It has no relation to: cause-and-association diagnosis
It is fundamentally characterized by: involves the immersive study of people and cultures in their natural environment over an extended period.
It is equivalent in purpose to: aims to describe the characteristics of a population or phenomenon being studied without manipulating variables.
It is correctly described as: determines the frequency with which something occurs or its association with something else, aiming to find causes of a phenomenon.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Diagnostic Research** is accurately described as: determines the frequency with which something occurs or its association with something else, aiming to find causes of a phenomenon.

slides.question: Q4: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Evaluative Research**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: aims to expand the existing body of scientific knowledge without any immediate practical application.
It has no relation to: worth and outcome assessment
It is correctly described as: is a systematic assessment of the worth or merit of a time-bound project, program, or policy.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is conducted for a research problem when there are few or no earlier studies to refer to, aiming to clarify the exact nature of the problem.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Evaluative Research** is accurately described as: is a systematic assessment of the worth or merit of a time-bound project, program, or policy.

slides.question: Q5: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Comparative Research**?
options: It has no relation to: similarity and difference analysis
It is equivalent in purpose to: involves studying and interpreting past events to understand current patterns and anticipate future trends.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is designed to solve practical, real-world problems of modern society.
It is correctly described as: focuses on identifying similarities and differences between two or more groups, subjects, or datasets.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Comparative Research** is accurately described as: focuses on identifying similarities and differences between two or more groups, subjects, or datasets.

slides.question: Q6: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Deductive Research Approach**?
options: It is correctly described as: starts with a general theory or hypothesis and tests it through specific empirical observations.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a reflective process of progressive problem-solving led by individuals working with others in teams to address immediate issues.
It has no relation to: top-down theory testing
It is equivalent in purpose to: relies on direct or indirect observation or experience as a database rather than theory or belief.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Deductive Research Approach** is accurately described as: starts with a general theory or hypothesis and tests it through specific empirical observations.

slides.question: Q7: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Inductive Research Approach**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: focuses on gathering numerical data and generalizing it across groups of people or to explain a particular phenomenon.
It is correctly described as: starts with specific observations or measurements and moves toward broader generalizations and theories.
It is equivalent in purpose to: focuses on the development of new theories or abstract models based on existing literature rather than direct experiments.
It has no relation to: bottom-up theory construction
slides.explanation: Correct! **Inductive Research Approach** is accurately described as: starts with specific observations or measurements and moves toward broader generalizations and theories.

slides.question: Q8: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Phenomenological Research**?
options: It has no relation to: lived experience description
It is fundamentally characterized by: is exploratory research used to gain an understanding of underlying reasons, opinions, and motivations.
It is correctly described as: is a qualitative approach that focuses on describing the lived experiences of individuals regarding a specific concept or phenomenon.
It is equivalent in purpose to: uses facts or information already available and analyzes them to make a critical evaluation of the material.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Phenomenological Research** is accurately described as: is a qualitative approach that focuses on describing the lived experiences of individuals regarding a specific concept or phenomenon.

slides.question: Q9: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Grounded Theory**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is related to some abstract idea(s) or theory, generally used by philosophers and thinkers to develop new concepts or to re-interpret existing ones.
It has no relation to: data-driven theory generation
It is fundamentally characterized by: aims to describe the characteristics of a population or phenomenon being studied without manipulating variables.
It is correctly described as: is a systematic qualitative methodology that involves the generation of theory from systematic analysis of data.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Grounded Theory** is accurately described as: is a systematic qualitative methodology that involves the generation of theory from systematic analysis of data.

slides.question: Q10: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Ethnographic Research**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is a category of research design in which the investigation starts after the fact has occurred without interference from the researcher.
It is correctly described as: involves the immersive study of people and cultures in their natural environment over an extended period.
It has no relation to: immersive cultural study
It is fundamentally characterized by: is conducted for a research problem when there are few or no earlier studies to refer to, aiming to clarify the exact nature of the problem.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Ethnographic Research** is accurately described as: involves the immersive study of people and cultures in their natural environment over an extended period.

slides.question: Q11: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Exploratory Design**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: states that there is a significant difference or relationship between variables, contrary to the null hypothesis.
It has no relation to: insight and familiarity collection
It is fundamentally characterized by: predicts that there is a relationship or difference, but does not specify which group will be higher or lower.
It is correctly described as: is selected when the primary objective is to gain insights and familiarity with a problem for more precise investigation later.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Exploratory Design** is accurately described as: is selected when the primary objective is to gain insights and familiarity with a problem for more precise investigation later.

slides.question: Q12: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Descriptive Design**?
options: It has no relation to: accurate characteristic profiling
It is equivalent in purpose to: occurs when the null hypothesis is rejected when it is actually true (false positive), denoted by alpha.
It is correctly described as: is chosen to provide an accurate portrayal of characteristics of a particular individual, situation, or group.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the extent to which a study establishes a trustworthy cause-and-effect relationship, free from confounding variables.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Descriptive Design** is accurately described as: is chosen to provide an accurate portrayal of characteristics of a particular individual, situation, or group.

slides.question: Q13: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Control Group**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: occurs when the null hypothesis is accepted when it is actually false (false negative), denoted by beta.
It has no relation to: untreated baseline group
It is correctly described as: is the group in an experiment that does not receive the treatment or manipulation, serving as a baseline for comparison.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the extent to which the results of a study can be generalized to other situations, people, and times.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Control Group** is accurately described as: is the group in an experiment that does not receive the treatment or manipulation, serving as a baseline for comparison.

slides.question: Q14: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Quasi-Experimental Design**?
options: It has no relation to: non-randomized comparison design
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the variable that is manipulated or controlled by the researcher to observe its effect.
It is correctly described as: is a research design that shares similarities with traditional experimental design but lacks random assignment to groups.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the conceptual blueprint or framework within which research is conducted.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Quasi-Experimental Design** is accurately described as: is a research design that shares similarities with traditional experimental design but lacks random assignment to groups.

slides.question: Q15: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Double-Blind Study**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is the variable being tested and measured, which changes in response to the independent variable.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a tentative, testable statement about the relationship between two or more variables.
It is correctly described as: is an experimental procedure in which neither the participants nor the researchers know who is receiving a particular treatment.
It has no relation to: participant-researcher bias prevention
slides.explanation: Correct! **Double-Blind Study** is accurately described as: is an experimental procedure in which neither the participants nor the researchers know who is receiving a particular treatment.

slides.question: Q16: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Statistical Power**?
options: It has no relation to: correct rejection probability (1-beta)
It is correctly described as: is the probability that a statistical test will correctly reject a false null hypothesis, equal to 1 minus beta.
It is fundamentally characterized by: asserts that there is no significant difference or relationship between the variables being tested.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is any variable other than the independent variable that could influence the results of the study.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Statistical Power** is accurately described as: is the probability that a statistical test will correctly reject a false null hypothesis, equal to 1 minus beta.

slides.question: Q17: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Directional Hypothesis**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is an extraneous variable that correlates with both the independent and dependent variables, masking the true relationship.
It has no relation to: expected relationship direction statement
It is correctly described as: specifies the expected direction of the relationship or difference between variables (e.g., group A will score higher than B).
It is fundamentally characterized by: states that there is a significant difference or relationship between variables, contrary to the null hypothesis.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Directional Hypothesis** is accurately described as: specifies the expected direction of the relationship or difference between variables (e.g., group A will score higher than B).

slides.question: Q18: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Non-Directional Hypothesis**?
options: It is correctly described as: predicts that there is a relationship or difference, but does not specify which group will be higher or lower.
It has no relation to: direction-free relationship statement
It is fundamentally characterized by: occurs when the null hypothesis is rejected when it is actually true (false positive), denoted by alpha.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a variable that is kept constant or controlled throughout the experiment to prevent it from influencing the outcome.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Non-Directional Hypothesis** is accurately described as: predicts that there is a relationship or difference, but does not specify which group will be higher or lower.

slides.question: Q19: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Internal Validity**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is a comprehensive summary of previous research on a specific topic, identifying gaps in current knowledge.
It is correctly described as: is the extent to which a study establishes a trustworthy cause-and-effect relationship, free from confounding variables.
It has no relation to: confound-free cause-effect trust
It is fundamentally characterized by: occurs when the null hypothesis is accepted when it is actually false (false negative), denoted by beta.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Internal Validity** is accurately described as: is the extent to which a study establishes a trustworthy cause-and-effect relationship, free from confounding variables.

slides.question: Q20: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **External Validity**?
options: It has no relation to: results generalization capability
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the variable that is manipulated or controlled by the researcher to observe its effect.
It is correctly described as: is the extent to which the results of a study can be generalized to other situations, people, and times.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a clear, precise statement identifying an area of concern, a condition to be improved, or a difficulty to be eliminated.
slides.explanation: Correct! **External Validity** is accurately described as: is the extent to which the results of a study can be generalized to other situations, people, and times.

slides.question: Q21: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Questionnaire**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: selects elements from an ordered population list at a regular interval (every k-th element) after a random starting point.
It is correctly described as: is a structured research instrument consisting of a series of questions for gathering information from respondents.
It has no relation to: standardized self-report tool
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the use of multiple data sources or collection methods to study a single phenomenon to increase validity.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Questionnaire** is accurately described as: is a structured research instrument consisting of a series of questions for gathering information from respondents.

slides.question: Q22: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Structured Interview**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: divides the population into naturally occurring groups (clusters) and randomly selects entire clusters to study.
It has no relation to: rigid standardized interview
It is correctly described as: is an interview method where all questions are pre-determined and asked in exactly the same order for all respondents.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the percentage of people who completed the research instrument out of the total sample selected.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Structured Interview** is accurately described as: is an interview method where all questions are pre-determined and asked in exactly the same order for all respondents.

slides.question: Q23: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Likert Scale**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is the trial administration of a questionnaire to a small group to detect and fix ambiguities before main collection.
It is correctly described as: is an interval scale used to measure attitudes by asking respondents to specify their level of agreement on a symmetric agree-disagree scale.
It has no relation to: measuring ordinal/interval agreement levels
It is equivalent in purpose to: involves selecting samples in stages using smaller and smaller sampling units at each stage.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Likert Scale** is accurately described as: is an interval scale used to measure attitudes by asking respondents to specify their level of agreement on a symmetric agree-disagree scale.

slides.question: Q24: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Semantic Differential Scale**?
options: It is correctly described as: is a scaling method that measures the psychological meaning of concepts using bipolar adjectives (e.g., Good-Bad).
It has no relation to: bipolar adjective scaling
It is equivalent in purpose to: does not use random selection, meaning some members of the population have zero chance of selection.
It is fundamentally characterized by: gives every element in the population an equal, non-zero chance of being selected.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Semantic Differential Scale** is accurately described as: is a scaling method that measures the psychological meaning of concepts using bipolar adjectives (e.g., Good-Bad).

slides.question: Q25: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Observation Method**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: selects samples that are easiest to access or recruit, resulting in high speed but potential bias.
It has no relation to: systematic behavior recording
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a probability sampling method where every member of the population has an equal chance of selection.
It is correctly described as: involves gathering primary data by systematically watching and recording behavioral patterns of people, objects, or occurrences.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Observation Method** is accurately described as: involves gathering primary data by systematically watching and recording behavioral patterns of people, objects, or occurrences.

slides.question: Q26: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Focus Group Discussion**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: divides the population into homogeneous subgroups (strata) and then samples randomly from each stratum.
It is correctly described as: is a qualitative technique where a small group of people (usually 6-10) discuss a topic guided by a moderator.
It has no relation to: moderated group discussion
It is equivalent in purpose to: selects a specified number of elements from defined subgroups, but using non-random selection methods.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Focus Group Discussion** is accurately described as: is a qualitative technique where a small group of people (usually 6-10) discuss a topic guided by a moderator.

slides.question: Q27: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Content Analysis**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: relies on the researcher's judgment to select elements that are most representative or informative for the study.
It is correctly described as: is a research tool used to determine the presence of certain words, themes, or concepts within qualitative data.
It has no relation to: qualitative data theme analysis
It is fundamentally characterized by: selects elements from an ordered population list at a regular interval (every k-th element) after a random starting point.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Content Analysis** is accurately described as: is a research tool used to determine the presence of certain words, themes, or concepts within qualitative data.

slides.question: Q28: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Data Triangulation**?
options: It has no relation to: multi-source validation technique
It is equivalent in purpose to: relies on existing research participants to recruit future participants from among their acquaintances.
It is correctly described as: is the use of multiple data sources or collection methods to study a single phenomenon to increase validity.
It is fundamentally characterized by: divides the population into naturally occurring groups (clusters) and randomly selects entire clusters to study.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Data Triangulation** is accurately described as: is the use of multiple data sources or collection methods to study a single phenomenon to increase validity.

slides.question: Q29: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Response Rate**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: occurs when the list from which the sample is drawn does not accurately represent the target population.
It has no relation to: completion ratio of sample
It is fundamentally characterized by: involves selecting samples in stages using smaller and smaller sampling units at each stage.
It is correctly described as: is the percentage of people who completed the research instrument out of the total sample selected.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Response Rate** is accurately described as: is the percentage of people who completed the research instrument out of the total sample selected.

slides.question: Q30: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Pilot Testing**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: does not use random selection, meaning some members of the population have zero chance of selection.
It has no relation to: questionnaire design validation
It is equivalent in purpose to: occurs when some members of a population are systematically more likely to be selected in a sample than others.
It is correctly described as: is the trial administration of a questionnaire to a small group to detect and fix ambiguities before main collection.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Pilot Testing** is accurately described as: is the trial administration of a questionnaire to a small group to detect and fix ambiguities before main collection.

slides.question: Q31: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Confidence Interval**?
options: It has no relation to: parameter estimation range
It is equivalent in purpose to: measures the dispersion or spread of data points around their arithmetic mean.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a non-parametric alternative to the independent t-test used to compare two independent groups.
It is correctly described as: is a range of values derived from sample statistics that is likely to contain the true population parameter.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Confidence Interval** is accurately described as: is a range of values derived from sample statistics that is likely to contain the true population parameter.

slides.question: Q32: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Degrees of Freedom**?
options: It is correctly described as: indicates the number of values in the final calculation of a statistic that are free to vary.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a non-parametric alternative to the paired t-test used to compare two related samples.
It has no relation to: independent observation count limit
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the average of the squared differences from the mean, representing the overall spread of data.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Degrees of Freedom** is accurately described as: indicates the number of values in the final calculation of a statistic that are free to vary.

slides.question: Q33: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Standard Error of the Mean**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a non-parametric alternative to one-way ANOVA used to compare three or more independent groups.
It is equivalent in purpose to: measures the strength and direction of a linear relationship between two continuous variables.
It has no relation to: sample mean precision estimate
It is correctly described as: measures the standard deviation of the sample mean's estimate of the population mean.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Standard Error of the Mean** is accurately described as: measures the standard deviation of the sample mean's estimate of the population mean.

slides.question: Q34: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Normal Distribution**?
options: It has no relation to: symmetric bell-curve model
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a non-parametric measure of rank correlation, assessing how well a relationship can be described using a monotonic function.
It is correctly described as: is a symmetric, bell-shaped probability distribution where the mean, median, and mode are all equal.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the mathematical average of a dataset, calculated by summing all values and dividing by the count.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Normal Distribution** is accurately described as: is a symmetric, bell-shaped probability distribution where the mean, median, and mode are all equal.

slides.question: Q35: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Positively Skewed**?
options: It has no relation to: right-tail distribution skew
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the middle value of an ordered dataset, dividing it into two equal halves, unaffected by outliers.
It is equivalent in purpose to: represents the rate of change in the dependent variable for every unit change in the independent variable.
It is correctly described as: is a distribution with a tail extending toward the right side (higher values), where Mean > Median > Mode.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Positively Skewed** is accurately described as: is a distribution with a tail extending toward the right side (higher values), where Mean > Median > Mode.

slides.question: Q36: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Negatively Skewed**?
options: It is correctly described as: is a distribution with a tail extending toward the left side (lower values), where Mean < Median < Mode.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the most frequently occurring value in a dataset.
It is equivalent in purpose to: makes strict assumptions about the parameters of the population distribution (e.g., normality).
It has no relation to: left-tail distribution skew
slides.explanation: Correct! **Negatively Skewed** is accurately described as: is a distribution with a tail extending toward the left side (lower values), where Mean < Median < Mode.

slides.question: Q37: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Kurtosis**?
options: It is correctly described as: measures the tail-heaviness or peakedness of a probability distribution relative to a normal distribution.
It is fundamentally characterized by: measures the dispersion or spread of data points around their arithmetic mean.
It is equivalent in purpose to: does not make assumptions about the underlying population distribution, suitable for ordinal or skewed data.
It has no relation to: peakedness or flatness score
slides.explanation: Correct! **Kurtosis** is accurately described as: measures the tail-heaviness or peakedness of a probability distribution relative to a normal distribution.

slides.question: Q38: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Mann-Whitney U Test**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is the average of the squared differences from the mean, representing the overall spread of data.
It has no relation to: distribution-free two-group comparison
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a non-parametric test used to determine if there is a significant association between two categorical variables.
It is correctly described as: is a non-parametric alternative to the independent t-test used to compare two independent groups.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Mann-Whitney U Test** is accurately described as: is a non-parametric alternative to the independent t-test used to compare two independent groups.

slides.question: Q39: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: measures the strength and direction of a linear relationship between two continuous variables.
It is correctly described as: is a non-parametric alternative to the paired t-test used to compare two related samples.
It has no relation to: distribution-free paired comparison
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a parametric test used to compare the means of three or more independent groups.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test** is accurately described as: is a non-parametric alternative to the paired t-test used to compare two related samples.

slides.question: Q40: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Kruskal-Wallis Test**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a non-parametric measure of rank correlation, assessing how well a relationship can be described using a monotonic function.
It is correctly described as: is a non-parametric alternative to one-way ANOVA used to compare three or more independent groups.
It has no relation to: distribution-free multi-group comparison
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a parametric test used to compare the means of exactly two groups, either independent or paired.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Kruskal-Wallis Test** is accurately described as: is a non-parametric alternative to one-way ANOVA used to compare three or more independent groups.

slides.question: Q41: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Abstract**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: occurs when an investigator's private interests or relationships compromise the professional judgment of research.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a formatting style by the Modern Language Association, commonly used in humanities, utilizing parenthetical page citations.
It has no relation to: brief paper summary overview
It is correctly described as: is a self-contained, brief summary of a research paper, thesis, or review, outlining goals, methods, and main findings.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Abstract** is accurately described as: is a self-contained, brief summary of a research paper, thesis, or review, outlining goals, methods, and main findings.

slides.question: Q42: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Footnote**?
options: It is correctly described as: is a note placed at the bottom of a page of a document that provides additional context or citations for text on that page.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a citation style providing bibliography and notes/author-date formats, used widely in history and social sciences.
It has no relation to: bottom-of-page contextual note
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the portion of a research paper dedicated to thanking individuals, funders, or institutions who aided the study.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Footnote** is accurately described as: is a note placed at the bottom of a page of a document that provides additional context or citations for text on that page.

slides.question: Q43: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Research Proposal**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is the standardized sequence of chapters (Introduction, Literature, Methods, Results, Discussion) for reporting research.
It has no relation to: planned research blueprint outline
It is equivalent in purpose to: are legal rights governing the protection of creations of the mind, including patents, copyrights, and trademarks.
It is correctly described as: is a structured document outlining what the researcher plans to study, why it is important, and how they will execute it.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Research Proposal** is accurately described as: is a structured document outlining what the researcher plans to study, why it is important, and how they will execute it.

slides.question: Q44: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Research Monograph**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is an exclusive right granted for an invention, providing the owner with the right to exclude others from making or selling it for a limited period.
It is correctly described as: is a specialist work of writing on a single scholarly subject or aspect of a subject, usually by a single author.
It has no relation to: single-subject specialist writing piece
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the practice of taking someone else's work or ideas and passing them off as one's own without proper citation.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Research Monograph** is accurately described as: is a specialist work of writing on a single scholarly subject or aspect of a subject, usually by a single author.

slides.question: Q45: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Citation Indexing**?
options: It is correctly described as: is the process of indexing publications by their citations to track scientific discourse and calculate metrics.
It has no relation to: tracking scientific citations system
It is fundamentally characterized by: occurs when an author reuses portions of their own previously published work without proper attribution.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a legal right that grants the creator of an original work exclusive rights for its use and distribution.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Citation Indexing** is accurately described as: is the process of indexing publications by their citations to track scientific discourse and calculate metrics.

slides.question: Q46: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Fabrication**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is a measure of the frequency with which the average article in a journal has been cited in a particular year.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a citation and formatting style established by the American Psychological Association, commonly used in social sciences.
It has no relation to: inventing non-existent research data
It is correctly described as: is the unethical practice of inventing data or results and recording or reporting them as true research outcomes.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Fabrication** is accurately described as: is the unethical practice of inventing data or results and recording or reporting them as true research outcomes.

slides.question: Q47: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Falsification**?
options: It has no relation to: manipulating active research data
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a formatting style by the Modern Language Association, commonly used in humanities, utilizing parenthetical page citations.
It is correctly described as: is the unethical practice of manipulating research materials, equipment, or processes, or changing or omitting data.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is an author-level metric that measures both the productivity and citation impact of the publications of a scientist.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Falsification** is accurately described as: is the unethical practice of manipulating research materials, equipment, or processes, or changing or omitting data.

slides.question: Q48: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Conflict of Interest**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is a committee formally designated to approve, monitor, and review biomedical and behavioral research involving humans.
It has no relation to: private interest compromising professional work
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a citation style providing bibliography and notes/author-date formats, used widely in history and social sciences.
It is correctly described as: occurs when an investigator's private interests or relationships compromise the professional judgment of research.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Conflict of Interest** is accurately described as: occurs when an investigator's private interests or relationships compromise the professional judgment of research.

slides.question: Q49: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Acknowledgment Section**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: are legal rights governing the protection of creations of the mind, including patents, copyrights, and trademarks.
It is correctly described as: is the portion of a research paper dedicated to thanking individuals, funders, or institutions who aided the study.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the process of providing research participants with clear info about the study to help them voluntarily agree to participate.
It has no relation to: thanking contributors and funders section
slides.explanation: Correct! **Acknowledgment Section** is accurately described as: is the portion of a research paper dedicated to thanking individuals, funders, or institutions who aided the study.

slides.question: Q50: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Thesis Layout Structure**?
options: It is correctly described as: is the standardized sequence of chapters (Introduction, Literature, Methods, Results, Discussion) for reporting research.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is an exclusive right granted for an invention, providing the owner with the right to exclude others from making or selling it for a limited period.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the evaluation of creative work or performance by other people in the same field in order to maintain quality.
It has no relation to: standardized academic chapter sequence
slides.explanation: Correct! **Thesis Layout Structure** is accurately described as: is the standardized sequence of chapters (Introduction, Literature, Methods, Results, Discussion) for reporting research.
